title: Russia-linked LNG Carrier ‘Merkuriy’ Loads at Arctic Hub, Expanding Shadow Fleet Capacity
content: The LNG carrier Merkuriy, recently reflagged to Russia alongside three other tankers, has been observed loading liquefied natural gas at the Saam floating storage unit (FSU), satellite imagery and automatic identification system (AIS) data show. The vessels are serving the sanctioned Arctic LNG 2 project.

title: Former Lauritzen boss to lead board of Danbulk
content: Martin Egvang has joined the newly formed Danish dry bulk operator Danbulk as its chairman of the board. Egvang was previously the CEO of Copenhagen-based shipowner and operator Lauritzen Bulkers. He stepped down from the role in March last year, citing stress issues. Before that, he was the CEO of another Danish operator, Integrity Bulk. …

title: Victoria commits $90m to progress offshore wind port at Port of Hastings
content: The Victoria state government has committed AUD 124.5m ($90.1m) in its 2026/27 budget to advance the proposed Renewable Energy Terminal at the Port of Hastings. This funding will progress the Environmental Effects Statement (EES) process for the first heavy-duty offshore wind port of its kind in Australia. The terminal is designed to enable onshore turbine …

title: One LNG transit, zero breakthrough in Hormuz crisis
content: Peace in the Persian Gulf appears no closer after president Donald Trump flatly rejected Iran’s response to a US peace proposal over the weekend, even as a rare transit by an LNG carrier offered a glimmer of hope that the Strait of Hormuz is not entirely sealed. “I have just read the response from Iran’s …

title: VLGC spot rates hit record highs
content: Very large gas carrier (VLGC) spot rates have surged to record levels, with benchmark freight on the Houston-to-Chiba route climbing to $290 per metric tonne and daily earnings spiking to almost $170,000 per day – the highest level ever recorded for the series, according to Clarksons Research. The record-breaking run has been driven by a …

title: Carbon costs, fuel choices and uncertainty shape Geneva Dry debate
content: The dry bulk sector’s decarbonisation debate has moved decisively beyond whether shipping will reach net zero and is now centred on how the industry can make the transition work commercially in an increasingly uncertain world. That was the clear message from the Dry Decarbonisation panel, the second session on day two of last month’s Geneva …

title: Vroon cashes out of livestock shipping business
content: Dutch shipowner Vroon is exiting the livestock shipping sector after agreeing to sell its Livestock Express business to Australian agribusiness and shipping group Heytesbury. The deal includes all 11 livestock carriers owned by Livestock Express, the Singapore-based operations and technical management activities for third-party vessels. Financial terms were not disclosed. The sale marks another step …

title: ONE returns to HD Hyundai for $1.2bn LNG-fuelled boxship deal
content: Ocean Network Express has returned to South Korea’s HD Hyundai Heavy Industries for another batch of LNG-capable containerships, pressing ahead with its long-term alternative-fuel fleet renewal strategy despite scaling back earlier expansion plans. Market sources identified the Singapore-headquartered liner operator as the buyer behind a $1.22bn newbuilding contract announced by HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore …

title: Japanese-built ultramax values jump
content: All eyes were on the Baltic Dry Index last week as it breached the 3,000-point barrier, its highest level in years, helping fuel another red-hot week in the sale and purchase market. Lion Shipbrokers reported 16 deals over the weekend, echoing comments from other broking houses, pointing to a strong appetite for 15-20-year-old Japanese-built panamax …

title: Awilco LNG changes course with CEO exit and board revamp
content: Norwegian gas carrier owner Awilco LNG is entering a new chapter after long-serving chief executive Jon Skule Storheill stepped down with immediate effect as the company prepares a broader leadership reshuffle. The Oslo-listed LNG shipping company said Storheill and the board had jointly agreed that the company should move into its next phase under new …

title: The seafarer shortage is not what shipping thinks it is
content: Kuba Szymanski, secretary-general of InterManager, writes for Splash today. Amid growing concern about a global seafarer shortage, many in the industry argue that recruitment is our biggest challenge. I disagree. The real issue is whether we are properly training, supporting and protecting the workforce we already have to build the industry’s future. For too long, …

title: EXCLUSIVE: Emirates cargo chief gets major new role after ‘challenging’ period
content:
Sources in the Middle East have reported that Nabil Sultan, formerly of Emirates SkyCargo and currently EVP passenger sales and country management, is to take on a new senior leadership role within the group, following the release of its 2025/6 financial results last week.
Mr Sultan led Emirates SkyCargo for 10 years, after replacing Ram Menem, before taking on the passenger role in 2024 – one well-placed source commented: “It’s a ...

title: FedEx bets on 30-year-old freighters…
content:
Key takeaway: The integrator plans to fly its MD-11 fleet again within weeks. A Kentucky lawmaker wants them grounded permanently. In the middle: a tightening global capacity gap and rates that just keep climbing (my previous related coverage follows: ‘The MD-11 broke early – and so did air cargo’s faith in old iron’). 
There is something almost poetic about the air cargo industry’s current predicament.
Middle East airspace closures initially knocked roughly ...<

title: Kuehne + Nagel sorely missing the ‘Otto factor’
content:
For years, Kuehne + Nagel (K+N) was the benchmark for execution in global forwarding – a company built on local entrepreneurship, strict accountability, and a culture that competitors tried (and largely failed) to replicate. Investors used to reward that model with a premium, if you talked to them… liking a lot a few key executives that were part of the management board. One above all.
Then…
… came August 2022.
Stefan Paul took ...

title: Brace for cost-out at DSV (and K+N) but how much?
content:
As this earnings season draws to a close – with the exception of Expeditors from Seattle… still adding to the workforce while delivering market-beating earnings – judging by Q1 26 interims, all leading asset-light freight forwarders are in belt-tightening mode.
And we ought to expect more of that in the near future.
Ahead of tomorrow – D(SV)-Day: Capital Markets Day (CMD), the full agenda can be downloaded here – several analysts have ...

title: Port stakeholders play the blame game at congestion-hit JNPA
content:
Indian container trade moving via terminals in Nhava Sheva Port (JNPA) are in danger of delays and missed connection, due to landside strains.
The congestion — simmering for weeks after a build-up of Middle East-related transhipment volumes — has resulted in finger-pointing among stakeholders on how to deal with the crisis.
Container trailer owners are struggling with a severe shortage of drivers, but they claim gate operational problems at terminals have compounded ...

content:
PRESS RELEASE 
DHL Express expands global portfolio with new Heavy Weight Express Service for shipments up to 3,000 kg
 – Heavy Weight Express enables global heavyweight express shipping up to 1,000 kg per piece / 3,000 kg per shipment
– Proactive shipment control through dedicated Heavy Weight Priority Desks ensures high reliability and customer service support
– DHL Express expands in the heavyweight segment, responding to growing demand
